revision.comment: create, borrowing code from [[Module:Navbar]]
revision.text:
-- This module functions a lot like {{error}}, but uses fancier css.
local p = {}
local HtmlBuilder = require('Module:HtmlBuilder')
local function _error(args) local s = args.message or args[1] or error('no message specified', 2) local tag = mw.ustring.lower(tostring(args.tag))
local t if tag == 'p' or tag == 'span' or tag == 'div' then t = tag else t = 'strong' end local root = HtmlBuilder.create(t)
root .addClass('error') .css('float', 'left') .css('white-space', 'nowrap') .wikitext('Error: ' .. tostring(s))
return tostring(root) end
function p.error(frame) local origArgs if frame == mw.getCurrentFrame() then -- We're being called via #invoke. If the invoking template passed any args, use -- them. Otherwise, use the args that were passed into the template. origArgs = frame:getParent().args for k, v in pairs(frame.args) do origArgs = frame.args break end else -- We're being called from another module or from the debug console, so assume -- the args are passed in directly. origArgs = frame end -- ParserFunctions considers the empty string to be false, so to preserve the previous -- behavior of the template, change any empty arguments to nil, so Lua will consider -- them false too.
local args = {} for k, v in pairs(origArgs) do if v ~= '' then args[k] = v end end
local args = origArgs return _error(args) end
return p

revision.comment: make it behave like [[Template:Error]]
revision.text:
-- This module implements {{error}}
local p = {}
local HtmlBuilder = require('Module:HtmlBuilder')
local function _error(args) local s = args.message or args[1] or error('no message specified', 2) local tag = mw.ustring.lower(tostring(args.tag))
local t if tag == 'p' or tag == 'span' or tag == 'div' then t = tag else t = 'strong' end local root = HtmlBuilder.create(t)
root .addClass('error') .wikitext(tostring(s))
return tostring(root) end
function p.error(frame) local origArgs if frame == mw.getCurrentFrame() then -- We're being called via #invoke. If the invoking template passed any args, use -- them. Otherwise, use the args that were passed into the template. origArgs = frame:getParent().args for k, v in pairs(frame.args) do origArgs = frame.args break end else -- We're being called from another module or from the debug console, so assume -- the args are passed in directly. origArgs = frame end -- ParserFunctions considers the empty string to be false, so to preserve the previous -- behavior of the template, change any empty arguments to nil, so Lua will consider -- them false too.
local args = {} for k, v in pairs(origArgs) do if v ~= '' then args[k] = v end end
local args = origArgs return _error(args) end
return p

revision.comment: test for all whitespace values rather than just the blank string
revision.text:
-- This module implements {{error}}
local p = {}
local HtmlBuilder = require('Module:HtmlBuilder')
local function _error(args) local s = args.message or args[1] or error('no message specified', 2) local tag = mw.ustring.lower(tostring(args.tag))
local t if tag == 'p' or tag == 'span' or tag == 'div' then t = tag else t = 'strong' end local root = HtmlBuilder.create(t)
root .addClass('error') .wikitext(tostring(s))
return tostring(root) end
function p.error(frame) local origArgs if frame == mw.getCurrentFrame() then -- We're being called via #invoke. If the invoking template passed any args, use -- them. Otherwise, use the args that were passed into the template. origArgs = frame:getParent().args for k, v in pairs(frame.args) do origArgs = frame.args break end else -- We're being called from another module or from the debug console, so assume -- the args are passed in directly. origArgs = frame end -- ParserFunctions considers the empty string to be false, so to preserve the previous -- behavior of the template, change any empty arguments to nil, so Lua will consider -- them false too.
local args = {} for k, v in pairs(origArgs) do if mw.ustring.match(v, '%S') then args[k] = v end end
local args = origArgs return _error(args) end
return p

revision.comment: more comments
revision.text:
-- This module implements {{error}}
local p = {}
local HtmlBuilder = require('Module:HtmlBuilder')
local function _error(args) local s = args.message or args[1] or error('no message specified', 2) local tag = mw.ustring.lower(tostring(args.tag))
-- Work out what html tag we should use. local t if tag == 'p' or tag == 'span' or tag == 'div' then t = tag else t = 'strong' end local root = HtmlBuilder.create(t)
-- Generate the html root .addClass('error') .wikitext(tostring(s))
return tostring(root) end
function p.error(frame) local origArgs if frame == mw.getCurrentFrame() then -- We're being called via #invoke. If the invoking template passed any args, use -- them. Otherwise, use the args that were passed into the template. origArgs = frame:getParent().args for k, v in pairs(frame.args) do origArgs = frame.args break end else -- We're being called from another module or from the debug console, so assume -- the args are passed in directly. origArgs = frame end -- ParserFunctions considers the empty string to be false, so to preserve the previous -- behavior of the template, change any empty arguments to nil, so Lua will consider -- them false too.
local args = {} for k, v in pairs(origArgs) do if mw.ustring.match(v, '%S') then args[k] = v end end
local args = origArgs return _error(args) end
return p

revision.comment: more comment tweaking
revision.text:
-- This module implements {{error}}.
local p = {}
local HtmlBuilder = require('Module:HtmlBuilder')
local function _error(args) local s = args.message or args[1] or error('no message specified', 2) local tag = mw.ustring.lower(tostring(args.tag))
-- Work out what html tag we should use. local t if tag == 'p' or tag == 'span' or tag == 'div' then t = tag else t = 'strong' end local root = HtmlBuilder.create(t)
-- Generate the html. root .addClass('error') .wikitext(tostring(s))
return tostring(root) end
function p.error(frame) local origArgs if frame == mw.getCurrentFrame() then -- We're being called via #invoke. If the invoking template passed any args, use -- them. Otherwise, use the args that were passed into the template. origArgs = frame:getParent().args for k, v in pairs(frame.args) do origArgs = frame.args break end else -- We're being called from another module or from the debug console, so assume -- the args are passed in directly. origArgs = frame end
-- ParserFunctions considers whitespace to be false, so to preserve the previous -- behavior of the template, change any arguments consisting only of whitespace -- to nil, so Lua will consider them false too. local args = {} for k, v in pairs(origArgs) do if mw.ustring.match(v, '%S') then args[k] = v end end
local args = origArgs return _error(args) end
return p

revision.comment: Protected Module:Error: [[WP:High-risk templates|Highly visible template or Lua module]] ([Edit=Block all non-admin users] (indefinite) [Move=Block all non-admin users] (indefinite))
revision.text:
-- This module implements {{error}}.
local p = {}
local HtmlBuilder = require('Module:HtmlBuilder')
local function _error(args) local s = args.message or args[1] or error('no message specified', 2) local tag = mw.ustring.lower(tostring(args.tag))
-- Work out what html tag we should use. local t if tag == 'p' or tag == 'span' or tag == 'div' then t = tag else t = 'strong' end local root = HtmlBuilder.create(t)
-- Generate the html. root .addClass('error') .wikitext(tostring(s))
return tostring(root) end
function p.error(frame) local origArgs if frame == mw.getCurrentFrame() then -- We're being called via #invoke. If the invoking template passed any args, use -- them. Otherwise, use the args that were passed into the template. origArgs = frame:getParent().args for k, v in pairs(frame.args) do origArgs = frame.args break end else -- We're being called from another module or from the debug console, so assume -- the args are passed in directly. origArgs = frame end
-- ParserFunctions considers whitespace to be false, so to preserve the previous -- behavior of the template, change any arguments consisting only of whitespace -- to nil, so Lua will consider them false too. local args = {} for k, v in pairs(origArgs) do if mw.ustring.match(v, '%S') then args[k] = v end end
local args = origArgs return _error(args) end
return p

revision.comment: simplify argument-grabbing code
revision.text:
-- This module implements {{error}}.
local p = {}
local HtmlBuilder = require('Module:HtmlBuilder')
local function _error(args) local s = args.message or args[1] or error('no message specified', 2) local tag = mw.ustring.lower(tostring(args.tag))
-- Work out what html tag we should use. local t if tag == 'p' or tag == 'span' or tag == 'div' then t = tag else t = 'strong' end local root = HtmlBuilder.create(t)
-- Generate the html. root .addClass('error') .wikitext(tostring(s))
return tostring(root) end
function p.error(frame) local origArgs if frame == mw.getCurrentFrame() then -- We're being called via #invoke. The args are passed through to the module -- from the template page, so use the args that were passed into the template. origArgs = frame.args else -- We're being called from another module or from the debug console, so assume -- the args are passed in directly. origArgs = frame end
-- ParserFunctions considers whitespace to be false, so to preserve the previous -- behavior of the template, change any arguments consisting only of whitespace -- to nil, so Lua will consider them false too. local args = {} for k, v in pairs(origArgs) do if mw.ustring.match(v, '%S') then args[k] = v end end
local args = origArgs return _error(args) end
return p

revision.comment: simplify code some more and trim whitespace from the message
revision.text:
-- This module implements {{error}}.
local p = {}
local HtmlBuilder = require('Module:HtmlBuilder')
local function _error(args) local message = args.message or args[1] or error('no message specified', 2) message = mw.ustring.match(tostring(message), '^%s*(.*%S)') or '' -- Convert message to string and trim whitespace. local tag = mw.ustring.lower(tostring(args.tag))
-- Work out what html tag we should use. if not (tag == 'p' or tag == 'span' or tag == 'div') then tag = 'strong' end
-- Generate the html. local root = HtmlBuilder.create(tag) root .addClass('error') .wikitext(message)
return tostring(root) end
function p.error(frame) local origArgs if frame == mw.getCurrentFrame() then -- We're being called via #invoke. The args are passed through to the module -- from the template page, so use the args that were passed into the template. origArgs = frame.args else -- We're being called from another module or from the debug console, so assume -- the args are passed in directly. origArgs = frame end
-- ParserFunctions considers whitespace to be false, so to preserve the previous -- behavior of the template, change any arguments consisting only of whitespace -- to nil, so Lua will consider them false too. local args = {} for k, v in pairs(origArgs) do if mw.ustring.match(v, '%S') then args[k] = v end end
return _error(args) end
return p

revision.comment: actually, don't trim whitespace, as the original template doesn't do this
revision.text:
-- This module implements {{error}}.
local p = {}
local HtmlBuilder = require('Module:HtmlBuilder')
local function _error(args) local message = args.message or args[1] or error('no message specified', 2) message = tostring(message) local tag = mw.ustring.lower(tostring(args.tag))
-- Work out what html tag we should use. if not (tag == 'p' or tag == 'span' or tag == 'div') then tag = 'strong' end
-- Generate the html. local root = HtmlBuilder.create(tag) root .addClass('error') .wikitext(message)
return tostring(root) end
function p.error(frame) local origArgs if frame == mw.getCurrentFrame() then -- We're being called via #invoke. The args are passed through to the module -- from the template page, so use the args that were passed into the template. origArgs = frame.args else -- We're being called from another module or from the debug console, so assume -- the args are passed in directly. origArgs = frame end
-- ParserFunctions considers whitespace to be false, so to preserve the previous -- behavior of the template, change any arguments consisting only of whitespace -- to nil, so Lua will consider them false too. local args = {} for k, v in pairs(origArgs) do if mw.ustring.match(v, '%S') then args[k] = v end end
return _error(args) end
return p

revision.comment: also don't bother removing whitespace values from the parameters, as the original template doesn't do this either
revision.text:
-- This module implements {{error}}.
local p = {}
local HtmlBuilder = require('Module:HtmlBuilder')
local function _error(args) local message = args.message or args[1] or error('no message specified', 2) message = tostring(message) local tag = mw.ustring.lower(tostring(args.tag))
-- Work out what html tag we should use. if not (tag == 'p' or tag == 'span' or tag == 'div') then tag = 'strong' end
-- Generate the html. local root = HtmlBuilder.create(tag) root .addClass('error') .wikitext(message)
return tostring(root) end
function p.error(frame) local origArgs if frame == mw.getCurrentFrame() then -- We're being called via #invoke. The args are passed through to the module -- from the template page, so use the args that were passed into the template. args = frame.args else -- We're being called from another module or from the debug console, so assume -- the args are passed in directly. args = frame end return _error(args) end
return p

revision.comment: fix variable name
revision.text:
-- This module implements {{error}}.
local p = {}
local HtmlBuilder = require('Module:HtmlBuilder')
local function _error(args) local message = args.message or args[1] or error('no message specified', 2) message = tostring(message) local tag = mw.ustring.lower(tostring(args.tag))
-- Work out what html tag we should use. if not (tag == 'p' or tag == 'span' or tag == 'div') then tag = 'strong' end
-- Generate the html. local root = HtmlBuilder.create(tag) root .addClass('error') .wikitext(message)
return tostring(root) end
function p.error(frame) local args if frame == mw.getCurrentFrame() then -- We're being called via #invoke. The args are passed through to the module -- from the template page, so use the args that were passed into the template. args = frame.args else -- We're being called from another module or from the debug console, so assume -- the args are passed in directly. args = frame end return _error(args) end
return p

revision.comment: convert message parameter to nil if it is blank
revision.text:
-- This module implements {{error}}.
local p = {}
local HtmlBuilder = require('Module:HtmlBuilder')
local function _error(args) local message = args.message or args[1] or error('no message specified', 2) message = tostring(message) local tag = mw.ustring.lower(tostring(args.tag))
-- Work out what html tag we should use. if not (tag == 'p' or tag == 'span' or tag == 'div') then tag = 'strong' end
-- Generate the html. local root = HtmlBuilder.create(tag) root .addClass('error') .wikitext(message)
return tostring(root) end
function p.error(frame) local args if frame == mw.getCurrentFrame() then -- We're being called via #invoke. The args are passed through to the module -- from the template page, so use the args that were passed into the template. args = frame.args else -- We're being called from another module or from the debug console, so assume -- the args are passed in directly. args = frame end -- if the message parameter is present but blank, change it to nil so that Lua will -- consider it false. if args.message and args.message == "" then args.message = nil end return _error(args) end
return p

revision.comment: Removed protection from "[[Module:Error]]": unprotecting - still bug fixes to be made
revision.text:
-- This module implements {{error}}.
local p = {}
local HtmlBuilder = require('Module:HtmlBuilder')
local function _error(args) local message = args.message or args[1] or error('no message specified', 2) message = tostring(message) local tag = mw.ustring.lower(tostring(args.tag))
-- Work out what html tag we should use. if not (tag == 'p' or tag == 'span' or tag == 'div') then tag = 'strong' end
-- Generate the html. local root = HtmlBuilder.create(tag) root .addClass('error') .wikitext(message)
return tostring(root) end
function p.error(frame) local args if frame == mw.getCurrentFrame() then -- We're being called via #invoke. The args are passed through to the module -- from the template page, so use the args that were passed into the template. args = frame.args else -- We're being called from another module or from the debug console, so assume -- the args are passed in directly. args = frame end -- if the message parameter is present but blank, change it to nil so that Lua will -- consider it false. if args.message and args.message == "" then args.message = nil end return _error(args) end
return p

revision.comment: simplify message input test
revision.text:
-- This module implements {{error}}.
local p = {}
local HtmlBuilder = require('Module:HtmlBuilder')
local function _error(args) local message = args.message or args[1] or error('no message specified', 2) message = tostring(message) local tag = mw.ustring.lower(tostring(args.tag))
-- Work out what html tag we should use. if not (tag == 'p' or tag == 'span' or tag == 'div') then tag = 'strong' end
-- Generate the html. local root = HtmlBuilder.create(tag) root .addClass('error') .wikitext(message)
return tostring(root) end
function p.error(frame) local args if frame == mw.getCurrentFrame() then -- We're being called via #invoke. The args are passed through to the module -- from the template page, so use the args that were passed into the template. args = frame.args else -- We're being called from another module or from the debug console, so assume -- the args are passed in directly. args = frame end -- if the message parameter is present but blank, change it to nil so that Lua will -- consider it false. if args.message == "" then args.message = nil end return _error(args) end
return p

revision.comment: Protected Module:Error: [[WP:High-risk templates|Highly visible template or Lua module]] ([Edit=Block all non-admin users] (indefinite) [Move=Block all non-admin users] (indefinite))
revision.text:
-- This module implements {{error}}.
local p = {}
local HtmlBuilder = require('Module:HtmlBuilder')
local function _error(args) local message = args.message or args[1] or error('no message specified', 2) message = tostring(message) local tag = mw.ustring.lower(tostring(args.tag))
-- Work out what html tag we should use. if not (tag == 'p' or tag == 'span' or tag == 'div') then tag = 'strong' end
-- Generate the html. local root = HtmlBuilder.create(tag) root .addClass('error') .wikitext(message)
return tostring(root) end
function p.error(frame) local args if frame == mw.getCurrentFrame() then -- We're being called via #invoke. The args are passed through to the module -- from the template page, so use the args that were passed into the template. args = frame.args else -- We're being called from another module or from the debug console, so assume -- the args are passed in directly. args = frame end -- if the message parameter is present but blank, change it to nil so that Lua will -- consider it false. if args.message == "" then args.message = nil end return _error(args) end
return p

revision.comment: Changed protection level of Module:Error: [[Wikipedia:Lua/Modules|High-risk Lua module]]: allow template editors ([Edit=Allow only template editors and admins] (indefinite) [Move=Allow only template editors and admins] (indefinite))
revision.text:
-- This module implements {{error}}.
local p = {}
local HtmlBuilder = require('Module:HtmlBuilder')
local function _error(args) local message = args.message or args[1] or error('no message specified', 2) message = tostring(message) local tag = mw.ustring.lower(tostring(args.tag))
-- Work out what html tag we should use. if not (tag == 'p' or tag == 'span' or tag == 'div') then tag = 'strong' end
-- Generate the html. local root = HtmlBuilder.create(tag) root .addClass('error') .wikitext(message)
return tostring(root) end
function p.error(frame) local args if frame == mw.getCurrentFrame() then -- We're being called via #invoke. The args are passed through to the module -- from the template page, so use the args that were passed into the template. args = frame.args else -- We're being called from another module or from the debug console, so assume -- the args are passed in directly. args = frame end -- if the message parameter is present but blank, change it to nil so that Lua will -- consider it false. if args.message == "" then args.message = nil end return _error(args) end
return p

revision.comment: use mw.html and other fixes from sandbox
revision.text:
-- This module implements {{error}}.
local p = {}
local function _error(args) local tag = mw.ustring.lower(tostring(args.tag))
-- Work out what html tag we should use. if not (tag == 'p' or tag == 'span' or tag == 'div') then tag = 'strong' end
-- Generate the html. return tostring(mw.html.create(tag) :addClass('error') :wikitext(tostring(args.message or args[1] or error('no message specified', 2))) ) end
function p.error(frame) local args if type(frame.args) == 'table' then -- We're being called via #invoke. The args are passed through to the module -- from the template page, so use the args that were passed into the template. args = frame.args else -- We're being called from another module or from the debug console, so assume -- the args are passed in directly. args = frame end -- if the message parameter is present but blank, change it to nil so that Lua will -- consider it false. if args.message == "" then args.message = nil end return _error(args) end
return p

revision.comment: Changed protection level of Module:Error: [[WP:High-risk templates|High-risk Lua module]]: only ~1200 transclusions ([Edit=Allow only autoconfirmed users] (indefinite) [Move=Allow only autoconfirmed users] (indefinite))
revision.text:
-- This module implements {{error}}.
local p = {}
local function _error(args) local tag = mw.ustring.lower(tostring(args.tag))
-- Work out what html tag we should use. if not (tag == 'p' or tag == 'span' or tag == 'div') then tag = 'strong' end
-- Generate the html. return tostring(mw.html.create(tag) :addClass('error') :wikitext(tostring(args.message or args[1] or error('no message specified', 2))) ) end
function p.error(frame) local args if type(frame.args) == 'table' then -- We're being called via #invoke. The args are passed through to the module -- from the template page, so use the args that were passed into the template. args = frame.args else -- We're being called from another module or from the debug console, so assume -- the args are passed in directly. args = frame end -- if the message parameter is present but blank, change it to nil so that Lua will -- consider it false. if args.message == "" then args.message = nil end return _error(args) end
return p

revision.comment: Changed protection level for "[[Module:Error]]": [[WP:High-risk templates|Highly visible template]] ([Edit=Require template editor access] (indefinite) [Move=Require template editor access] (indefinite))
revision.text:
-- This module implements {{error}}.
local p = {}
local function _error(args) local tag = mw.ustring.lower(tostring(args.tag))
-- Work out what html tag we should use. if not (tag == 'p' or tag == 'span' or tag == 'div') then tag = 'strong' end
-- Generate the html. return tostring(mw.html.create(tag) :addClass('error') :wikitext(tostring(args.message or args[1] or error('no message specified', 2))) ) end
function p.error(frame) local args if type(frame.args) == 'table' then -- We're being called via #invoke. The args are passed through to the module -- from the template page, so use the args that were passed into the template. args = frame.args else -- We're being called from another module or from the debug console, so assume -- the args are passed in directly. args = frame end -- if the message parameter is present but blank, change it to nil so that Lua will -- consider it false. if args.message == "" then args.message = nil end return _error(args) end
return p

revision.comment: 1 revision imported from [[:wikipedia:Module:Error]]: importing template
revision.text:
-- This module implements {{error}}.
local p = {}
local function _error(args) local tag = mw.ustring.lower(tostring(args.tag))
-- Work out what html tag we should use. if not (tag == 'p' or tag == 'span' or tag == 'div') then tag = 'strong' end
-- Generate the html. return tostring(mw.html.create(tag) :addClass('error') :wikitext(tostring(args.message or args[1] or error('no message specified', 2))) ) end
function p.error(frame) local args if type(frame.args) == 'table' then -- We're being called via #invoke. The args are passed through to the module -- from the template page, so use the args that were passed into the template. args = frame.args else -- We're being called from another module or from the debug console, so assume -- the args are passed in directly. args = frame end -- if the message parameter is present but blank, change it to nil so that Lua will -- consider it false. if args.message == "" then args.message = nil end return _error(args) end
return p

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was 18 revisions imported from [[:wikipedia:Module:Error]]
revision.text:
-- This module implements {{error}}.
local p = {}
local function _error(args) local tag = mw.ustring.lower(tostring(args.tag))
-- Work out what html tag we should use. if not (tag == 'p' or tag == 'span' or tag == 'div') then tag = 'strong' end
-- Generate the html. return tostring(mw.html.create(tag) :addClass('error') :wikitext(tostring(args.message or args[1] or error('no message specified', 2))) ) end
function p.error(frame) local args if type(frame.args) == 'table' then -- We're being called via #invoke. The args are passed through to the module -- from the template page, so use the args that were passed into the template. args = frame.args else -- We're being called from another module or from the debug console, so assume -- the args are passed in directly. args = frame end -- if the message parameter is present but blank, change it to nil so that Lua will -- consider it false. if args.message == "" then args.message = nil end return _error(args) end
return p

revision.comment: allow accessing from other modules
revision.text:
-- This module implements {{error}}.
local p = {}
function p._error(args) local tag = mw.ustring.lower(tostring(args.tag))
-- Work out what html tag we should use. if not (tag == 'p' or tag == 'span' or tag == 'div') then tag = 'strong' end
-- Generate the html. return tostring(mw.html.create(tag) :addClass('error') :cssText(args.style) :wikitext(tostring(args.message or args[1] or error('no message specified', 2))) ) end
function p.error(frame) local args if type(frame.args) == 'table' then -- We're being called via #invoke. The args are passed through to the module -- from the template page, so use the args that were passed into the template. args = frame.args else -- We're being called from another module or from the debug console, so assume -- the args are passed in directly. args = frame end -- if the message parameter is present but blank, change it to nil so that Lua will -- consider it false. if args.message == "" then args.message = nil end return p._error(args) end
return p

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: Bot: reimport from enwiki to fix erroneous transclusions [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23208182735|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
-- This module implements {{error}}.
local p = {}
function p._error(args) local tag = mw.ustring.lower(tostring(args.tag))
-- Work out what html tag we should use. if not (tag == 'p' or tag == 'span' or tag == 'div') then tag = 'strong' end
-- Generate the html. return tostring(mw.html.create(tag) :addClass('error') :cssText(args.style) :wikitext(tostring(args.message or args[1] or error('no message specified', 2))) ) end
function p.error(frame) local args if type(frame.args) == 'table' then -- We're being called via #invoke. The args are passed through to the module -- from the template page, so use the args that were passed into the template. args = frame.args else -- We're being called from another module or from the debug console, so assume -- the args are passed in directly. args = frame end -- if the message parameter is present but blank, change it to nil so that Lua will -- consider it false. if args.message == "" then args.message = nil end return p._error(args) end
return p

revision.comment: allow accessing from other modules
revision.text:
-- This module implements {{error}}.
local p = {}
function p._error(args) local tag = mw.ustring.lower(tostring(args.tag))
-- Work out what html tag we should use. if not (tag == 'p' or tag == 'span' or tag == 'div') then tag = 'strong' end
-- Generate the html. return tostring(mw.html.create(tag) :addClass('error') :cssText(args.style) :wikitext(tostring(args.message or args[1] or error('no message specified', 2))) ) end
function p.error(frame) local args if type(frame.args) == 'table' then -- We're being called via #invoke. The args are passed through to the module -- from the template page, so use the args that were passed into the template. args = frame.args else -- We're being called from another module or from the debug console, so assume -- the args are passed in directly. args = frame end -- if the message parameter is present but blank, change it to nil so that Lua will -- consider it false. if args.message == "" then args.message = nil end return p._error(args) end
return p

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: overwriting
revision.text:

function p._error(args) local tag = mw.ustring.lower(tostring(args.tag))
-- Work out what html tag we should use. if not (tag == 'p' or tag == 'span' or tag == 'div') then tag = 'strong' end
-- Generate the html. return tostring(mw.html.create(tag) :addClass('error') :cssText(args.style) :wikitext(tostring(args.message or args[1] or error('no message specified', 2))) ) end
function p.error(frame) local args if type(frame.args) == 'table' then -- We're being called via #invoke. The args are passed through to the module -- from the template page, so use the args that were passed into the template. args = frame.args else -- We're being called from another module or from the debug console, so assume -- the args are passed in directly. args = frame end -- if the message parameter is present but blank, change it to nil so that Lua will -- consider it false. if args.message == "" then args.message = nil end return p._error(args) end
return p

-- This module implements {{error}}.
local p = {}
function p._error(args) local tag = mw.ustring.lower(tostring(args.tag))
-- Work out what html tag we should use. if not (tag == 'p' or tag == 'span' or tag == 'div') then tag = 'strong' end
-- Generate the html. return tostring(mw.html.create(tag) :addClass('error') :cssText(args.style) :wikitext(tostring(args.message or args[1] or error('no message specified', 2))) ) end
function p.error(frame) local args if type(frame.args) == 'table' then -- We're being called via #invoke. The args are passed through to the module -- from the template page, so use the args that were passed into the template. args = frame.args else -- We're being called from another module or from the debug console, so assume -- the args are passed in directly. args = frame end -- if the message parameter is present but blank, change it to nil so that Lua will -- consider it false. if args.message == "" then args.message = nil end return p._error(args) end
return p

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: overwriting
revision.text:
-- This module implements {{error}}.
local p = {}
function p._error(args) local tag = mw.ustring.lower(tostring(args.tag))
-- Work out what html tag we should use. if not (tag == 'p' or tag == 'span' or tag == 'div') then tag = 'strong' end
-- Generate the html. return tostring(mw.html.create(tag) :addClass('error') :cssText(args.style) :wikitext(tostring(args.message or args[1] or error('no message specified', 2))) ) end
function p.error(frame) local args if type(frame.args) == 'table' then -- We're being called via #invoke. The args are passed through to the module -- from the template page, so use the args that were passed into the template. args = frame.args else -- We're being called from another module or from the debug console, so assume -- the args are passed in directly. args = frame end -- if the message parameter is present but blank, change it to nil so that Lua will -- consider it false. if args.message == "" then args.message = nil end return p._error(args) end
return p
